product.name: ArWoWear 330 Moda Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Modacrylic Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
product.specLine: 330 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
descriptions: Antistatic 3/1S Twill Fabric 60% modacrylic 38% cotton 2% carbon, inherent flame retardant antistatic twill fabric . ArWoWear 330 Moda Pro fabric are mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 4 to 5.
ArWoWear 330 Moda Pro are inherent flame retardant with EN 11612 certification.
Suitable for antistatic application, certified for EN ISO 1149.
It provides arc flash protection with EN 61482 class 1 (4 kA) certification.
60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
330 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
technicalParameters: Yarn | Warp:30/2 Ne; Weft:30/2 Ne | EN ISO 2060
Tensile Strength | Warp > 1000N ; Weft > 800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 25N ; Weft > 25N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

evidence: mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicitly stated use by welders and certified to EN ISO 11611 welding protection standard.

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Flame Retardant High Visible Jacket and Trouser Modacrylic
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicitly certified to EN ISO 20471 high-visibility standard and marketed with hi-vis jacket/trouser application imagery.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicitly described for electrical workers with arc flash protection certification, directly connecting to high-voltage electrical work.
Product description explicitly lists firefighters as an intended user group alongside heat/flame protection certification.

evidence: mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly stated to be used by welders and is certified to EN ISO 11611, the dedicated welding protection standard, making welding the most specific and directly supported end use.

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Flame Retardant High Visible Jacket and Trouser Modacrylic
secondary.rationale: The product is independently certified to EN ISO 20471 and explicitly marketed with high-visibility jacket/trouser imagery, supporting a genuine secondary hivis application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Firefighters are mentioned as users but no firefighting-specific standard like EN 469 is cited; welding certification is more explicit and dominant.
Electrical workers and arc protection are mentioned, but EN 1149 antistatic and EN 61482 arc rating alone are insufficient per rules without a clearer electrical-sector framing than welding/hi-vis.
A narrower, explicitly named sector (welding) dominates over generic industrial workwear classification.
